Reports indicate that Persona — the identity verification service provider for Discord — has suffered a security breach. Any user who has submitted ID documents through this system may be at risk of sensitive data leaks. What does this mean? * Potential exposure of official ID photos. * Increased risks of identity theft. * A surge in targeted phishing attempts. Users are strongly advised to monitor any official notifications, keep an eye on their financial accounts, and enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) immediately.

I recently visited the stunning Sardhana Church, also known as the Basilica of Our Lady of Graces, about 30 km from my home. ᵂⁱᵈᵉ ˢʰᵒᵗ I go there often, and every time I get amazed watching its beauty. Let me accept that this church is hundreds of years old, so despite all the maintenance by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI), a government body, it looks old. Let me share what I felt as I arrived at this grand white marble structure on a sunny afternoon, stepping through massive gates into a world frozen in time. ᴳʳᵒᵘⁿᵈ ˢʰᵒᵗ * The building you are seeing in this image is Sardhana Church, or Basilica of Our Lady of Graces, which serves as a Catholic shrine dedicated to the Virgin Mary. Its main function is to grace Marian devotion through the venerated Sacred Image of Our Lady of Graces, considered the church's most illustrious ornament and a site of reported miracles. ᴰⁱᶠᶠᵉʳᵉⁿᵗ ᵃⁿᵍˡᵉ It hosts daily Holy Masses at 6 AM in summer and at 7 AM in winter, and also multiple times on Sunday services, drawing pilgrims from India and abroad, especially in November for annual events. Incidentally, I arrived in November 2025, especially to go to this church. ᴰⁱᶠᶠᵉʳᵉⁿᵗ ᵃⁿᵍˡᵉ As a minor basilica nominated by Pope John XXIII in 1961, it remains North India's largest church and a protected monument since 1924. The church can accommodate 40 people at a time, so people wait here for their turn in the next mass. By the way, this church is not just a prayer spot, but it shows Begum Samru's legacy, the history, the architecture, her faith, and of course the religious education via the nearby seminary and schools. They all run in buildings donated by Begum Samru. You can see a pilgrimage center, which accommodates distinguished visitors and acts as an information center also. ˢⁱⁿᵃᵍᵉ ᵃⁿᵈ ᶜᵘˢᵗᵒᵐˢ   * Built by Begum Samru, a Muslim woman who converted to Christianity, it all started after her husband died in 1778. I would like to mention here that Begum Samru’s husband, Walter Reinhardt Sombre, was an Austrian mercenary soldier, a European who had his own army that he used for fighting for local conflicts in India on a payment basis. ᴬⁿᵒᵗʰᵉʳ ᵃⁿᵍˡᵉ In 1767, he married Farzana, a 14-year-old girl, a dancer, who later became known as Begum Samru. After Walter Reinhardt Sombre's death in 1778, Begum Samru took over his mercenary army and ruled the Sardhana estate for 55 years. Once she inherited the Sardhana estate and ruled like a queen, but one day she felt like honoring the Virgin Mary with this church. ᴬ ˢʰᵒᵗ ᶠʳᵒᵐ ᵖᵃʳᵏⁱⁿᵍ ᵃʳᵉᵃ The construction started in or around 1810 and finished by 1822, costing a large amount, which would be in billions today. As I walk inside, I always feel proud of the Italian architect Antonio Raghellini, from Padua, who supervised it all. There are several marble statues of Jesus, Mary, and saints which were originally carved in Italy, and brough here by ship and would you believe these were brought to this distant place from Kolkata that was Calcutta back then by bullock carts? Now I show you a couple of the 14 Stations of the Cross statues on the way to church from the gate, which show you the legacy and Begum Samru's faith.   Begum Samru tried her best and asked the Pope to make Sardhana its own diocese. In 1834, Pope Gregory XVI created the Apostolic Vicariate here, with Bishop Julius Caesar Scotti as the first vicar in this church for some time. Later they merged this church into Agra's vicariate. Finally, in 1961, Pope John XXIII honored it as a minor basilica for its beauty and history, one of India's selected few. ​ ᴾᵉᵒᵖˡᵉ ᶜᵒᵐᵉ ᵗᵒ ᵖʳᵃʸ ʰᵉʳᵉ ​To be frank, I go to every place that has architectural value but not as a devotee, including churches, temples, mosques, and other religious places like Gurudwaras, Jain, and Boudh temples, but just to appreciate their beauty. So I never enter the main buildings but watch them from outside. The same is the case here. However, watching the devotion of pilgrims praying to a miraculous Mary at this place felt great to me. By the way, Begum Samru donated her old palace, which is now a seminary, the bishop's house, and a girls' school close to this church, which I will show you some other time. ᴵⁿᶠᵒʳᵐᵃᵗⁱᵒⁿ ᶜᵉⁿᵗᵉʳ * Whenever I visit Sardhana Church, I find it peaceful as always. People come here mainly to pray and feel close to God. As I walked in and watched from a distance, families knelt before the statue of Our Lady of Graces, lighting candles and whispering prayers for health or family. Many touch it gently, believing it brings miracles and I've heard stories of healings. Everyone joins the Holy Mass. on Sundays, crowds fill the pews for services starting early until noon. I stayed for one hour or so and heard people sing hymns. People come here to confess sins to priests or say the rosary in groups, especially on Saturdays. ˢⁱⁿᵃᵍᵉ ᵃⁿᵈ ᶜᵘˢᵗᵒᵐˢ   But as I said I wander the marble hall, snapping photos of Italian statues, the dome, and 14 Cross stations outside. You can see pilgrims picnic by the gardens and compound, kids play nearby, and some visit Begum Samru's old palace turned seminary. I try to visit in November, when thousands flock for the big festival mood. For me it's a mix of history walks, watching people pray, and quiet reflection perfect for the soul. ʸᵒᵘʳˢ ᵗʳᵘˡʸ Video Link ### Location Details |Basilica of Our Lady of Grace, Church| Sardhana, India | |-------------|--------| |Place Type | Church | |Visiting Hours | Morning To Noon } |Days open | All Days/ Sundays Special mass | |Google Maps | | |Atlas-Map PIN | [//]:# (!steematlas 29.14750626 lat 77.6165843 long Basilica of Our Lady of Grace, Sardhana d3scr) | Thank you to the organizers of this challenge. If you have spent any time on tech Twitter or LinkedIn lately, you have probably seen the term being thrown around like a hot potato. It sounds like something a teenager would say while trying to explain their latest TikTok hobby. However, in the world of high-stakes software engineering, vibe coding is becoming the definitive shift in how we build, scale, and think about products. At Jhavtech Studios, we have been watching this evolution closely. For years, the industry focused on the syntax, the semicolons, and the precise architecture of every class. Today, the conversation is shifting from the "how" of writing lines to the "what" of the intent. ## What exactly is Vibe Coding? To put it simply, it is the process of building software where the developer acts more like a conductor than a pianist. Instead of manually typing out every function, you are steering an AI model by communicating the feel, the logic, and the desired outcome. You are coding based on the vibe of the project rather than the grueling manual labor of the keyboard. This is not just for beginners. In fact, some of the most prominent voices in tech, including Andrej Karpathy, have noted that as AI models become more sophisticated, the bottleneck is no longer the ability to write code but the ability to describe a vision. When we talk about AI-assisted software development, we are talking about a partnership where the human provides the intuition and the machine provides the raw output. ### The Senior Developer’s Secret Weapon You might think that senior developers would be the first to roll their eyes at the idea of . After all, they spent decades mastering the craft. But the opposite is true. The most seasoned engineers are the ones embracing this because they have the one thing AI lacks: taste. A senior dev knows when a piece of software feels right. They understand the edge cases and the architectural pitfalls that a junior might miss. When you use a tool like the Cursor AI IDE, you aren't just letting a bot write code; you are using a high-powered lever to multiply your output. Senior developers are moving away from being "code monkeys" and transitioning into "system architects" who can see the big picture. ## Why Vibe Coding vs Traditional Programming for Senior Developers Matters There is a heated debate right now regarding vibe coding vs traditional programming for senior developers. Traditionalists argue that if you aren't manually managing memory or writing your own unit tests from scratch, you aren't really "coding." However, the statistics tell a different story. According to a , 92 percent of developers are already using AI coding tools in some capacity. Furthermore, a study by McKinsey found that AI-enabled software development can speed up certain coding tasks by as much as 35 to 45 percent. For a senior engineer, that means more time spent on high-level strategy and less time on boilerplate. In a professional environment, this transition is crucial. For instance, when we handle a , we often find that the original team got bogged down in technical debt and "spaghetti code." By applying modern principles, a senior dev can use AI to quickly refactor and reorganize a failing codebase, focusing on the architectural "vibe" that will actually sustain the business long-term. ## The Power of Intent-Based Development We are moving toward an era of intent-based development. This means the IDE understands what you want to achieve before you even finish the sentence. If you are building a fintech app, the "vibe" requires security, speed, and reliability. You don't want to spend three days setting up a basic API authentication flow. You want to prompt the AI to build it according to industry standards and then spend your energy on the unique logic that makes your app special. ### Practical Applications: Rapid Prototyping and MVPs One of the most effective ways to use this new methodology is in the early stages of a startup. Knowing how to use vibe coding for rapid prototyping and MVPs is a superpower for modern founders. In the past, building a Minimum Viable Product (MVP) took months. Now, with the right prompts and a clear vision, you can have a working prototype in a weekend. This is where Jhavtech Studios excels. We help businesses take those raw ideas and turn them into scalable realities. By leveraging these modern workflows, we reduce the time to market without sacrificing the quality that an enterprise-grade product requires. ## The Role of Agentic IDEs The tools are evolving just as fast as our habits. We are seeing the rise of Agentic IDEs, which are environments where the AI does not just suggest the next word but actually performs tasks autonomously. Imagine telling your editor to "update all the deprecated dependencies in this folder and fix the resulting errors." That is the reality of the modern workflow. ## Addressing the Risks: It is not all Magic Is vibe coding perfect? Absolutely not. If you do not have a solid foundation in computer science, you can easily "vibe" your way into a technical disaster. This is why a is more important now than ever before. When an AI generates 500 lines of code in five seconds, someone needs to actually understand what those lines are doing. A senior developer’s job has shifted from writing the code to auditing it. If you lack the experience to spot a logic flaw, the AI will happily lead you off a cliff. ### The Enterprise Perspective: Benefits of Vibe Coding in Enterprise Environments Many large-scale companies are hesitant to adopt these tools due to security and privacy concerns. However, the benefits of vibe coding in enterprise environments are too large to ignore. From automating documentation to generating complex SQL queries, the efficiency gains are massive. According to a , by 2028, 75 percent of enterprise software engineers will use AI code assistants. The companies that resist this change will find themselves outpaced by smaller, more agile teams that have mastered the art of "vibe" and "intent." ## Is Vibe Coding Replacing Senior Software Engineers? This is the question that keeps many people up at night: Is vibe coding replacing senior software engineers? The short answer is no. But it is replacing the version of a senior engineer who refuses to adapt. The role is evolving from a builder to a director. We are seeing a shift where the "soft skills" of engineering — communication, empathy for the user, and system design — are becoming the most valuable assets. If you look at the services offered at Jhavtech Studios, you will notice a focus on end-to-end solutions. This is because we know that code is just a means to an end. Whether we are building a custom mobile app or a complex web platform, the goal is to solve a business problem. Vibe coding allows us to get to that solution faster and with more creative freedom. ## Final Thoughts... Embracing the Future The reality is that vibe coding is just the next logical step in the abstraction of programming. We moved from machine code to assembly, from assembly to C, and from C to high-level languages like Python and JavaScript. Each step was met with skepticism by the "old guard," and each step eventually became the standard. For senior developers, this is not a threat; it is an invitation. It is an invitation to stop worrying about the syntax of a loop and start worrying about the impact of the software. It is about reclaiming your time to focus on what actually matters. As we move through 2026, the gap between those who can "vibe" with their tools and those who are stuck in the manual past will only widen. The future belongs to the developers who can bridge the gap between human creativity and machine execution. If you are looking to bring your next big idea to life, or if you need a team that understands how to navigate this new landscape, visit us at Jhavtech Studios. We don't just write code; we build the future, one vibe at a time.

More Investment in Artificial Intelligence. Towards physical AI. In China, the company Limex Dynamics invested more than $200 million in a single round to create robots with integrated intelligence, machines and devices for the body and brain as a single system. This way you can connect to distant servers, the robot itself will participate in movement, balance, etc. Contact with objects and interaction with the environment are the specialists in the matter, and above all artificial intelligence. At the center of this e-strategy is a platform designed by Cosa, described by Limex as the nervous system for humanoid robots, the COSA functions as a cognitive operating system capable of controlling the robot's entire body in real life, arms, legs, torso, balance and object balance manipulation. The company compares this architectural design to the human brain, the brain region is responsible for coordinating fine and fluid movements. This means that the robot does not only execute pre-programmed sequences, it understands the context of what it is doing. This level of autonomy is the result of the integration between COSA and the company's second major technology, the Tron 2 platform, a modular system of limbs, joints and torso that allows different types of robberies to be assembled from the same base. The main Limex humanoid called Oli is 1.65 cm tall, weighs about 55 kg and has arms with 7 degrees of freedom each, capable of carrying up to 10 kg and can already overcome obstacles, walk on moderately irregular terrain, recognize small objects and grab them with precision. In a recently released video, 18 OLI robots appear emerging from shipping containers on their own. getting up, processing information and executing synchronized movements, is a clear portrait of where robotics is heading, no more machines specialized in a single task, but general platforms capable of learning new behaviors over time. For Limex Dynamics, this funding is not just about building better robots, it is about accelerating the creation of a new type of intelligence, an intelligence that is born from the body, that learns by walking, wandering, balancing and touching. If this path continues to advance at the current pace, the future of robotics will not be defined by machines that only obey, but by machines that understand the world through their own experience. When that brain begins to exist, the body also needs to accompany it and that is where the physical limits begin to fall. En realidad, lo que queda no es polvo gris ligero. Son fragmentos minerales de tus huesos. Calcio. Fósforo. Restos cristalizados de lo que alguna vez fue tu estructura. Durante la cremación, el fuego alcanza temperaturas de hasta 1000 grados Celsius. Los órganos desaparecen. La piel se evapora. Pero los huesos… resisten. Y aquí viene lo más impactante. Ese material mineral puede transformarse en algo completamente diferente. Hoy en día, mediante tecnología de alta presión, el carbono de un cuerpo humano puede convertirse en un diamante real. When you stroll through a Mediterranean garden or pass a sun‑drenched sidewalk, you’re likely to be greeted by the glossy, lance‑shaped leaves and clusters of fragrant blooms that belong to the oleander (Nerium oleander). This hardy shrub, cultivated for centuries across warm climates, offers a striking display of pink, white, red, or even bi‑colored flowers that seem to flourish from early spring until the first frost . In just 400 words, let’s explore why oleander remains a favorite of gardeners, what makes its blossoms so resilient, and the cultural meanings that have grown up around them. A Quick Botanical Snapshot Oleander belongs to the Dogbane family (Apocynaceae) and is native to the dry regions of the Mediterranean, North Africa, and parts of Asia. Its evergreen habit, deep‑rooted system, and drought‑tolerant foliage make it a perfect candidate for xeriscaping—a landscaping style that conserves water. The flowers themselves are tubular, five‑petaled, and arranged in panicles that can hold dozens of blossoms per stem. Their sweet, honey‑like scent attracts pollinators such as bees and butterflies, while the plant’s glossy leaves provide a striking backdrop all year long. Why the Flowers Shine Year‑Round Heat & Sun Tolerance – Oleander thrives under full sun, converting intense radiation into vigorous growth rather than stress. Low Water Needs – Once established, the shrub can survive on minimal irrigation, making it ideal for regions where water is scarce. Disease Resistance – Its thick cuticle repels many common fungal pathogens, so the blooms stay healthy even in humid conditions. Because of these traits, the flowers often appear continuously, with new buds forming as older ones fade. Pruning just after the first flush of blooms encourages a second wave, extending the visual display into late summer. Care Tips for a Flourishing Oleander Planting Site – Choose a spot with at least six hours of direct sunlight and well‑draining soil. Watering – Water deeply at planting, then let the soil dry out between sessions; over‑watering can lead to root rot. Fertilizing – A balanced, slow‑release fertilizer applied in early spring and again in midsummer will support abundant flowering. Pruning – Trim back 20–30 % after the initial bloom to shape the plant and stimulate a second flowering cycle. Safety Note: All parts of the oleander contain potent cardiac glycosides. Wear gloves when handling cuttings and keep the plant away from children and pets. Symbolism and Cultural Roots Oleander’s vibrant blossoms have long been associated with both admiration and caution. In ancient Greece, it symbolized resilience and beauty amidst adversity, while in some Middle‑Eastern cultures it represented caution, reflecting its toxic nature. Today, many gardeners celebrate oleander as a “living jewel” that brings color to otherwise barren, sun‑baked landscapes. Bringing Oleander into Your Garden Whether you’re designing a low‑maintenance backyard oasis or adding a splash of color to a commercial façade, oleander offers an effortless combination of elegance and durability. By respecting its water‑wise nature, pruning for continuous bloom, and handling it responsibly, you can enjoy a cascade of fragrant flowers that brighten any summer scene. Embrace the timeless allure of oleander—where beauty blooms, even in the harshest of suns. ***

শুধরে দিতে ভুলবেন না। পৃথিবীতে সবচেয়ে অমূল্য কোনো কিছু যদি আমরা বলে থাকি, কিন্তু আমার মতামতে যদি পৃথিবীর কোনো কিছু অমূল্য হিসেবে ধরা যায়, তবে তার মধ্যে প্রথমে যেটা আমার কাছে আসবে বলে মনে হয়, সেটা হলো মায়ের হাতের রান্না করা খাবার। কারণ সেটা শুধুমাত্র একটা খাবার হয় না, সেটা হয় মমতা, ভালোবাসা, শ্রদ্ধা এবং দুশ্চিন্তা। দুশ্চিন্তা বললাম বলে হয়তো অনেকেই এখন ভাববেন, “মায়ের খাবার কী করে দুশ্চিন্তা হয়?” আসলে আমি যেই নজরে কথাটি বলছি, আমি যে চিন্তা-চেতনায় কথাটি বলছি, সেটা হলো আমাদের মায়েরা কিন্তু সব সময় আমাদের নিয়ে দুশ্চিন্তা করেন, এবং সেটা রান্নার মধ্যেও একটা ছাপ ফেলে যায়। অর্থাৎ, এটা খুব ভালো করেই খেয়াল করবেন যে, প্রতিটি রান্না করার সময় উনারা খুব ভালোভাবেই চিন্তা করেন যে, এই রান্নাটি আমাদের জন্য কেমন হবে, কিংবা আসলে খাবারটি আমাদের ভালো লাগবে কিনা, আমাদের শরীরের জন্য কেমন হবে, আমাদের মজা লাগবে কিনা অনেক অনেক দুশ্চিন্তার মধ্য দিয়ে তিনি আমাদের জন্য খাবারটি রান্না করেন। এবং আমরা খাওয়া পর্যন্ত দুশ্চিন্তা করতে থাকেন যে, আমরা খাবারটি শেষ করলাম কিনা। কিন্তু তার বিপরীতে, যদি আমরা আমাদের ইউনিভার্সিটি কিংবা অফিসের ক্যান্টিনের কথা চিন্তা করি, তাহলে কিন্তু সেটা একেবারেই ভিন্ন। কারণ চিন্তা করে দেখুন, আমাদের অফিস কিংবা ইত্যাদি জায়গার যে ক্যান্টিনের খাবারগুলো, অর্থাৎ ফাস্টফুডগুলো আমরা খাই, সেগুলো কিন্তু মোটেও স্বাস্থ্যসম্মত নয়। আর তার বিপরীতে আমাদের মায়েদের খাবারগুলো অসম্ভব স্বাস্থ্যসম্মত হয়। শুধু তাই নয়, আসলে আমরা যে এই ফাস্টফুডগুলো খাই, এগুলো হয় অতিরিক্ত আনহাইজেনিক, যেটা আমাদের শরীরের পক্ষে একেবারেই ক্ষতিকর। এবং এই খাবারগুলো আমরা যখন খাই, তখন কিন্তু আমাদের শরীর দিন দিন অনেক বেশি খারাপ হতে থাকে। এই কথাগুলো আসলে এজন্যই বললাম আমরা জানি কোনটা খারাপ এবং আমরা এটাও জানি যে, কোনটা ভালো। কিন্তু আমরা আসলে ভালো-খারাপ আলাদা করতে পারা সত্ত্বেও সেদিকে নজর দেই না, এবং আমরা আসলে আমাদের নিজেদের ক্ষতি করতেই থাকি, করতেই থাকি। এবং আমরা আসলে এক পর্যায়ে গিয়ে সেটা উপলব্ধি করতে পারি, কিন্তু ততদিন অনেক দেরি হয়ে যায়।

*** # 探索四季的变化 自然界的四季变化是一个令人着迷的主题，每个季节都有其独特的魅力和特征。在这篇博客中，我们将深入探索春、夏、秋、冬四个季节的美丽与奇妙。 ## 春天：万物复苏 春天是生命重新焕发活力的季节。随着气温的回升，花朵竞相绽放，树木重新披上绿装。这个季节象征着希望和新生。 ### 春天的特点 - **温暖的气候**：春天的气温逐渐升高，适合户外活动。 - **花卉盛开**：樱花、郁金香等花卉在此时争相开放。 - **动物活动**：许多动物在春天繁殖，鸟儿开始筑巢。 ## 夏天：热情似火 夏天是热情洋溢的季节，阳光灿烂，万物生机盎然。人们常常选择在这个季节进行户外活动，享受阳光和海滩。 ### 夏天的特点 - **高温天气**：夏季气温普遍较高，适合游泳和野外露营。 - **丰收季节**：许多水果和蔬菜在夏天成熟，市场上琳琅满目。 - **丰富的活动**：夏季是举办音乐节、露天电影等活动的最佳时机。 ## 秋天：丰收与沉思 秋天是一个丰收的季节，树叶变得五彩斑斓，空气中弥漫着果实的香甜。这个季节常常让人感到宁静和沉思。 ### 秋天的特点 - **凉爽的天气**：气温逐渐下降，适合进行长途徒步旅行。 - **丰富的果实**：苹果、南瓜等水果和蔬菜在此时成熟。 - **自然的变色**：树叶从绿色变为红、黄、橙等色彩，形成美丽的秋景。 ## 冬天：静谧与温暖 冬天是一个静谧的季节，覆盖着雪的世界显得格外宁静。虽然气温寒冷，但冬天也有其独特的温暖和节日气氛。 ### 冬天的特点 - **寒冷的天气**：气温骤降，适合滑雪和冰上活动。 - **节日庆典**：圣诞节和新年等节日让这个季节充满欢声笑语。 - **温暖的家庭聚会**：冬季是与家人团聚的好时机，热腾腾的食物和温暖的饮品让人感到温馨。 ## 结论 四季的变化不仅丰富了我们的自然景观，也影响着我们的生活方式和心情。无论是春天的生机、夏天的热情、秋天的沉思，还是冬天的宁静，每个季节都有其独特的魅力。让我们一起走出门，去探索和感受大自然的美丽吧！ *** ***

হ্যালো বন্ধুরা, সবাই কেমন আছেন। আশা করি আল্লাহর রহমতে সবাই অনেক ভাল এবং সুস্থ আছেন। আমিও আলহামদুলিল্লাহ আল্লাহর রহমতে খুবই ভাল আছি। প্রতিদিনের মতো আজকেও আপনাদের সামনে নতুন একটি পোস্ট নিয়ে হাজির হলাম। কিন্তু আজকে আমি একেবারে নতুন একটি পোস্ট নিয়ে আসলাম। যেটা হচ্ছে ফটোগ্রাফি পোস্ট। ফটোগ্রাফি করতে এখন আমার কাছে ভীষণ ভালো লাগে। এজন্য আমি যেখানে যাই ফটোগ্রাফি করতে পছন্দ করি। এজন্য চিন্তা করলাম ভিন্ন ভিন্ন ধরনের কিছু ফটোগ্রাফি শেয়ার করব। আসলে আমি মনে করি ফটোগ্রাফি করাটা একটা আর্ট। সে ক্ষেত্রে ফটোগ্রাফি করা কিন্তু অতটাও সহজে বিষয় নয়। কিন্তু তারপরেও সময় দিয়ে ফটোগ্রাফি সুন্দর করার চেষ্টা করি। আমি নিজেই কিছু ফটোগ্রাফি করে নিলাম। আশা করি আমার ফটোগ্রাফি গুলো আপনাদের ভালো লাগবে। ফটোগ্রাফি - ১ : এটি হচ্ছে বন্য ফুলের ফটোগ্রাফি। বন্য ফুল গুলো দেখতে কিন্তু বেশ ভালো লাগে। তবে বন্য ফুল গুলো অযত্নে অবহেলা যেখানে সেখানে বড় হয়। যত্ন ছাড়া হলেও এ ফুলগুলো কিন্তু দেখতে কিন্তু বেশ ভালো লাগে। যদিও এই বন্যফুল আকার অনেক ছোট। তবে গ্রাম অঞ্চলের রাস্তার পাশে জমির পাশে এই বন্যফুল গুলো দেখা যায়। আর গাছের মধ্যে কিন্তু এই ফুল গুলো অনেক ফোটে। ছোট ছোট গাছের মধ্যে ছোট ছোট ফুল দেখতেও চমৎকার লাগে। কিছুদিন আগে আমাদের বাড়ির সামনে থেকে আমি এই ফুলের ফটোগ্রাফি করেছিলাম। তাই আজকে বন্য ফুলের ফটোগ্রাফি আমি আপনাদের মাঝে শেয়ার করলাম। device : Samsung galaxy a7 ফটোগ্রাফি - ২ : এটি হচ্ছে গ্রামের রাস্তার ফটোগ্রাফি। গ্রামের রাস্তাগুলো কিন্তু চিকন আর দুই পাশে কিন্তু ঘাস এবং গাছ থাকে। আর এই রোড গুলো দেখতেও কিন্তু খুব ভালো লাগে। কারণ গ্রামের রাস্তার প্রাকৃতিক সৌন্দর্য উপভোগ করা যায়। যদিও এখন সব জায়গাতে গণ বসতি দেখা যায়। তারপরও গ্রামের রাস্তাগুলোর মধ্যে আলাদা একটা সৌন্দর্য লুকিয়ে থাকে। কিছুদিন আগে আমি ঘুরতে গিয়ে একটি বাড়ির সামনের এই রাস্তার ফটোগ্রাফি করেছিলাম। তাই আজকে এই ফটোগ্রাফি আমি আপনাদের মাঝে শেয়ার করলাম। device : Samsung galaxy a7 ফটোগ্রাফি - ৩ : এটি হচ্ছে কাঁচা মরিচের ফটোগ্রাফি। কাঁচা মরিচ চিনে না এমন মানুষ নেই বললেই চলে। কাঁচা মরিচ এমন একটি জিনিস যেকোনো রান্নার কাজে এবং কিছু খেতে এগুলোর প্রয়োজন হয়। যদিও রমজান কাঁচা কাঁচা মরিচের দাম কিন্তু অনেক। তবে গ্রাম অঞ্চলে কিন্তু কাঁচা মরিচ দেখা যায় অনেক। অনেক অনেক জায়গা থেকে কিন্তু আবার কাঁচামরিচ সংগ্রহ করে বিক্রি করে। কিছুদিন আগে আমাদের বাড়ির সামনে এক লোক কাঁচামরিচ বিক্রি করার জন্য আসলো। আর কাঁচা মরিচ গুলো দেখে আমার কাছে অনেক ভালো লাগলো। আমি কিছু কাঁচামরিচ ওইখান থেকে কিনেছিলাম।তখন আমি এই কাঁচামরিচ এর ফটোগ্রাফি করেছিলাম। তাই আজকে কাঁচামরিচের ফটোগ্রাফি আমি আপনাদের মাঝে শেয়ার করলাম। device : Samsung galaxy a7 আমার নাম আকলিমা আক্তার মুনিয়া। আর আমার ইউজার নাম @bdwomen। আমি বাংলাদেশে বসবাস করি। বাংলা ভাষা হল আমাদের মাতৃভাষা আর আমি মাতৃভাষা বলতে পারি বলেই অনেক গর্বিত। আমি বিভিন্ন ধরনের ছবি এবং পেইন্টিং আঁকতে খুবই পছন্দ করি। আমি প্রায় সময় বিভিন্ন ধরনের পেইন্টিং এঁকে থাকি। আবার রঙিন পেপার এবং বিভিন্ন রকমের জিনিস দিয়ে নানা ধরনের কারুকাজ তৈরি করতে আমার খুবই ভালো লাগে। আবার নিজের বিভিন্ন জায়গায় গিয়ে ছবি তুলতে খুবই ভালো লাগে। আমি চেষ্টা করি সব ধরনের জিনিস কখনো না কখনো একবার করে করার জন্য। আবার বিভিন্ন ধরনের আইডিয়া মাথায় আসলে সেগুলো ও করার চেষ্টা করি। ধন্যবাদ সবাইকে

The Digital Renaissance, Techno Crusades 2026 The speed at which technology has evolved has never been this rapid in 2026. Technology is now part of our daily lives, changing our work, creativity and communication from the time of living in “science fiction.” Now, with the advent of AI and the internet‘s decentralization, we witness our “Digital Renaissance.“ca part of the song of what is to come for mankind. The Rise of Generative Intelligence This year, the biggest revolution has been the evolution of Generative AI. It is no longer a technology in the toy aisle but the co, pilot of human creativity. From CAD (3D architectural drawings) to generating strange but photorealistic images of advanced engines, AI brings automation to the creation process. Generativity democratizes that ability because now if it can be imagined, it can be created by anyone. This revolution allows millions of creators on Steemit to have a voice, to publish to the world. Connectivity Without Borders Beyond AI, 2026 is the year of “almost” limitless connectivity. As advanced satellite internet scales and the Metaverse is improved, distance is no longer a hindrance to communication. Education and commerce are happening in innovative 3D worlds, exchanging ideas in a way that the best video conference call could not replicate. We are no longer “using” the internet but “living” inside it. Final Words: Defining A Future of Compassionate Innovation Despite all of these actions and understandings, the challenge is to do business by the human rules. This is to ensure technology does not become a barrier to real world experience but an enhancement to bring us better opportunities. If we maintain the flow of information and adapt to the change, the future for everyone will be a bright digital tomorrow.

*** # বন্ধুত্বের রঙ একটি ছোট্ট গ্রামে ছিল দুই বন্ধু, রাহুল এবং সুমি। তারা ছোটবেলা থেকে একসাথে বড় হয়েছে। রাহুল ছিল খুবই চঞ্চল এবং সুমি ছিল শান্ত ও ভাবুক। তারা একে অপরের সাথে সবসময় সময় কাটাতো, খেলাধুলা করতো এবং নতুন নতুন স্বপ্ন দেখতো। একদিন, রাহুলের মনে হলো, তারা যদি নিজেদের বন্ধুত্বের রঙ তৈরি করে, তাহলে তাদের বন্ধুত্ব আরও মজবুত হবে। সে সুমিকে বলল, "চলো, আমরা আমাদের বন্ধুত্বের জন্য একটি রঙ তৈরি করি।" সুমি প্রথমে একটু অবাক হলো, কিন্তু পরে সে রাজি হলো। তারা দুজন মিলে একটি বড় ক্যানভাস কিনলো এবং রং নিয়ে আসলো। রাহুল বললো, "আমরা আমাদের বন্ধুত্বের রঙ তৈরি করবো।" তারা প্রথমে রাঙা রং দিয়ে একটি বড় হৃদয় আঁকলো। রাহুল বললো, "এই রঙ আমাদের ভালোবাসা এবং আনন্দের প্রতীক।" এরপর তারা নীল রং দিয়ে আকাশ আঁকলো। সুমি বললো, "এই রঙ আমাদের স্বপ্ন এবং আশা।" তারা আরও বিভিন্ন রঙ ব্যবহার করে ক্যানভাসটি পূর্ণ করলো। হলুদ রং দিয়ে তারা সূর্য আঁকলো, যা তাদের উজ্জ্বল ভবিষ্যতের প্রতীক। সবশেষে, তারা সব রঙের মিশ্রণে একটি সুন্দর বর্ণালী তৈরি করলো, যা তাদের বন্ধুত্বের বৈচিত্র্যকে প্রতিফলিত করলো। কিছুদিন পর, গ্রামে একটি মেলা অনুষ্ঠিত হলো। রাহুল এবং সুমি তাদের তৈরি ক্যানভাস নিয়ে সেখানে গেলো। সবাই তাদের কাজটি দেখে মুগ্ধ হলো। গ্রামের লোকেরা বললো, "এটি সত্যিই অসাধারণ! বন্ধুত্বের রঙ আমাদেরও প্রেরণা দেয়।" রাহুল এবং সুমি বুঝতে পারলো, তাদের বন্ধুত্বের রঙ শুধু তাদের জন্য নয়, বরং পুরো গ্রামের জন্য একটি উৎসাহের উৎস হতে পারে। তারা সিদ্ধান্ত নিলো, তারা এই ক্যানভাসকে গ্রামের কেন্দ্রস্থলে স্থাপন করবে, যাতে সবাই এটি দেখতে পারে এবং বন্ধুত্বের গুরুত্ব বুঝতে পারে। গ্রামের মানুষ তাদের কাজের জন্য ধন্যবাদ জানালো এবং রাহুল ও সুমির বন্ধুত্বের রঙ পুরো গ্রামে ছড়িয়ে পড়লো। তাদের বন্ধুত্বের রঙ সত্যিই একটি নতুন অধ্যায়ের সূচনা করলো, যেখানে সবাই একে অপরের সাথে বন্ধুত্ব ও ভালোবাসা ভাগাভাগি করতে শিখলো। এভাবেই রাহুল এবং সুমির বন্ধুত্বের রঙ চিরকালীন হয়ে রইলো, তাদের গ্রামে একটি উজ্জ্বল আলো হয়ে। *** ***

Source To understand the significance of this advance, we must first understand the limits of light. A standard optical microscope cannot see objects smaller than half the wavelength of the light it uses. This is known as the Abbe Limit. In a conventional optical microscope, the absolute limit is approximately 200 to 250 nanometers (nm), and if you try to see something smaller (such as a moving atom or electron), the light simply passes through or is scattered. >Para poder entender lo que supone este avance, primero hay que entender cuales son los límites de la luz. Un microscopio óptico normal no puede ver objetos más pequeños que la mitad de la longitud de onda de la luz que usa. Esto se conoce como el Límite de Abbe. En un microscopio óptico convencional, el límite absoluto es de aproximadamente 200 a 250 nanómetros (nm) y si intentas ver algo más pequeño (como un átomo o un electrón en movimiento), la luz simplemente "pasa de largo" o se difumina. This is because light behaves like a wave. When light passes through a lens, it diffracts (scatters). If you try to observe two points that are closer than 200 nm to each other, their diffraction patterns overlap so much that the eye (or camera) sees them as a single blur. To give you an idea of ​​where each technique stands, with optical microscopy, the smallest we can observe are bacteria with an average size of about 2,000 nm. Super-resolution techniques, such as electron and scanning tunneling microscopes, often require staining the sample with fluorescent dyes, which are frequently toxic or distort what you are seeing. >Esto se debe a que la luz se comporta como una onda. Cuando la luz pasa por una lente, se difracta (se dispersa). Si intentas observar dos puntos que están más cerca de 200 nm entre sí, sus patrones de difracción se solapan tanto que el ojo (o la cámara) los ve como un solo borrón. Para hacernos una idea de dónde se sitúa cada técnica, con la microscopía óptica lo más que podemos llegar a observar son bacterias que tienen un tamaño medio de unos 2.000 nm. Existen técnicas de super-resolución como los microscopios electrónicos y de efecto túnel suelen requerir teñir la muestra con colorantes fluorescentes que a menudo son tóxicos o alteran lo que estás viendo. Source The quantum microscope developed by MIT (Massachusetts Institute of Technology) aims to achieve nanometer or even picometer-scale resolutions (a thousand times smaller than a nanometer) without the need for dyes. It directly observes the interaction of "compressed" light or electrons with matter, allowing scientists to "see" where light, due to pure physics, should not be able to reach. Two major milestones are the terahertz microscope and the resolved atom microscope. >El microscopio cuántico desarrollado por el MIT (Massachusetts Institute of Technology) intenta alcanzar resoluciones de escala nanométrica o incluso picométrica (mil veces más pequeño que un nano) sin necesidad de colorantes, observando directamente la interacción de la luz "comprimida" o de los electrones con la materia, lo que permite "ver" donde la luz, por pura física, no debería poder llegar. Principalmente, hablamos de dos grandes hitos: "el microscopio de terahercios" y la "microscopía de átomos resueltos". Terahertz radiation lies between microwaves and infrared. Its long wavelength would theoretically result in terrible resolution; however, MIT has managed to "compress" it using spintronic emitters (which use the electron's spin, not just its charge). By placing the sample extremely close to the emitter, they "trap" the light before it propagates and scatters. It's like trying to paint a miniature with a thick brush—you usually can't—but if you force the bristles through a microscopic hole just before they touch the canvas, you can achieve a fine brushstroke. >La radiación de terahercios está entre las microondas y el infrarrojo. Su longitud de onda es larga, lo que en teoría daría una resolución pésima, sin embargo el MIT ha logrado "comprimirla", utilizando emisores espintrónicos (que usan el giro del electrón, no solo su carga). Al colocar la muestra extremadamente cerca del emisor, "atrapan" la luz antes de que se propague y se disperse. Es como intentar pintar una miniatura con un pincel gordo, normalmente no puedes, pero si obligas a las cerdas a pasar por un agujero microscópico justo antes de tocar el lienzo, consigues un trazo fino. Source As for "frozen" atom microscopy, this advance allows scientists to see individual atoms interacting freely, something that quantum mechanics usually obscures behind the Uncertainty Principle. The process is carried out in three steps. First, they keep a cloud of ultracold atoms floating, allowing them to collide and interact naturally. Suddenly, they activate an optical lattice (light grid) that locks the atoms into their exact positions in a fraction of a second. Finally, a laser makes them glow so that a high-resolution camera can photograph them one by one. >En cuanto a la microscopía de átomos "congelados", este avance permite ver átomos individuales interactuando libremente, algo que la mecánica cuántica suele ocultar tras el Principio de Incertidumbre. El proceso se realiza en 3 pasos. Primero mantienen una nube de átomos ultrafríos flotando, permitiéndoles chocar e interactuar de forma natural. De golpe, activan una retícula óptica (red de luz) que bloquea a los átomos en su posición exacta en una fracción de segundo. Finalmente, un láser los hace brillar para que una cámara de alta resolución pueda fotografiarlos uno a uno. I suppose you're thinking this is all very interesting, but what the heck is it good for us mere mortals? Well, with these microscopes, for example, we can see exactly how lithium ions move and where they get "stuck" in the batteries we use every day. This would allow us to improve battery technology to the point where we could have cell phones that charge in 2 minutes or electric car batteries that last 20 years instead of 8. It will also be possible to manufacture medical sensors so sensitive that they can detect a disease before symptoms appear. >Supongo que estaréis pensando que esto es muy bonito pero, ¿para que coño nos sirve a los mortales? Pues con estos microscopios por ejemplo, podemos ver exactamente cómo se mueven los iones de litio y dónde se "atascan" en las baterías que utilizamos diariamente. Esto permitiría mejorar la tecnología de las baterías hasta poder tener móviles que se cargan en 2 minutos o baterías de coches eléctricos que duran 20 años en lugar de 8. También se podrán fabricar sensores médicos tan sensibles que detectan una enfermedad antes de que haya síntomas. More information/Más información

Source At the beginning of the 20th century, Edison designed a nickel-iron (NiFe) battery, an extremely durable and robust rechargeable battery characterized by the use of nickel oxide in the cathode, iron in the anode, and an alkaline electrolyte (potassium hydroxide). It is notable for its ability to withstand deep discharges, overcharging, and physical abuse, with a lifespan exceeding 20-30 years. Edison proposed it for electric vehicles at the beginning of the 20th century because it was very robust and durable compared to lead-acid batteries, but relatively heavy and with a lower energy density. >A principios del siglo XX, Edison diseñó una batería de níquel-hierro (NiFe), una batería recargable extremadamente duradera y robusta, caracterizada por usar óxido de níquel en el cátodo, hierro en el ánodo y un electrolito alcalino (hidróxido de potasio). Destaca por soportar descargas profundas, sobrecargas y abusos físicos, con una vida útil que supera los 20-30 años. Edison la propuso para vehículos eléctricos a comienzos del siglo XX porque era muy robusta y duradera frente a las de plomo‑ácido, pero relativamente pesada y con menor densidad de energía. The battery was heavy, inefficient, and had a high self-discharge rate, and I suppose that partly because of that and partly because of the success of the internal combustion engine, this battery faded into obscurity. But now a team of researchers at the University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) has taken that classic chemistry and recreated it with new nanomaterials to achieve ultra-fast charging and an enormous lifespan, intended primarily for stationary renewable energy storage. >La batería era pesada, ineficiente y con una alta tasa de auto descarga y supongo que en parte por eso y en parte por el éxito de motor de explosión, esta batería se quedó dormida en la historia. Pero ahora un equipo de investigadores de la Universidad de California Los Angeles (UCLA) ha tomado esa química clásica y la ha recreado con nuevos nano-materiales para lograr cargas ultrarrápidas y una vida útil enorme, que está pensada sobre todo para almacenamiento estacionario de renovables. Source In a traditional Ni-Fe battery, during discharge, metallic iron at the iron electrode (anode) is oxidized to iron(II) hydroxide, releasing electrons into the external circuit. At the nickel electrode (cathode), nickel(II) hydroxide is reduced/oxidized between Ni(II) and Ni(III) states, accepting electrons. The alkaline electrolyte (KOH) transports the ions within the cell, while the electrons travel through the external circuit, powering the charge. During charging, the process is reversed: iron is forced back to its most reduced state and nickel to its most oxidized state. >En una Ni‑Fe tradicional, durante la descarga, en el electrodo de hierro (ánodo) el hierro metálico se oxida a hidróxido de hierro, liberando electrones al circuito externo. En el electrodo de níquel (cátodo) el hidróxido de níquel se reduce/oxida entre estados de Ni(II)/Ni(III), aceptando electrones. El electrolito alcalino (KOH) transporta los iones dentro de la celda, mientras los electrones viajan por el circuito externo alimentando la carga. En la carga el proceso se invierte: se fuerza a que el hierro vuelva a su estado más reducido y el níquel a su estado más oxidado. ​ The international group co-led by UCLA has redesigned the same basic chemistry using metallic nanoclusters and ultrathin carbon supports for the electrodes. They grow extremely small clusters of nickel or iron using proteins as a “template” and embed them in a carbon-based conductor only a few atoms thick. These nanoclusters offer a huge electrochemically active surface and very short paths for ion and electron transport, allowing charging and discharging in seconds instead of hours. >El grupo internacional codirigido por UCLA ha rediseñado la misma química básica usando nano-cúmulos metálicos y soportes de carbono ultrafinos para los electrodos. Crecen cúmulos de níquel o hierro extremadamente pequeños usando proteínas como “plantilla” y los incrustan en un conductor basado en carbono de sólo unos átomos de espesor. Estos nanoclústeres ofrecen una superficie electroquímicamente activa enorme y caminos muy cortos para el transporte de iones y electrones, lo que permite cargar y descargar en cuestión de segundos en lugar de horas. Source The prototype exceeds 12,000 full charge-discharge cycles, equivalent to more than 30 years of daily recharging, without significant degradation. While a typical household lithium battery might be designed for 3,000–5,000 useful cycles, this reimagined Ni-Fe battery targets tens of thousands of cycles, sacrificing energy per unit mass in exchange for longevity and charging speed. This battery does not directly compete with lithium-ion batteries for electric cars because its energy density remains lower. >El prototipo supera los 12.000 ciclos completos de carga‑descarga, equivalente a más de 30 años de recargas diarias, sin degradarse de forma significativa. Mientras una batería doméstica típica de litio quizá esté diseñada para 3.000–5.000 ciclos útiles, esta Ni‑Fe re-imaginada apunta a decenas de miles de ciclos, sacrificando energía por unidad de masa a cambio de longevidad y velocidad de carga. Esta batería no compite directamente con las baterías de ion‑litio para coches eléctricos porque su densidad de energía sigue siendo menor. But it's well-suited for stationary storage in solar farms that charge during the day and discharge at night, or data centers that need immediate backup when the grid goes down. Using abundant materials (nickel, iron, carbon, proteins) and a lifespan of decades can lower the overall cost of renewable storage infrastructure compared to technologies that need to be replaced more frequently. In short, they've "resurrected" Edison's robust, low-impact chemistry, but using nanotechnology and biotemplates to create an extremely fast and almost "eternal" battery in terms of cycles, designed as an energy storage solution for the renewable energy transition rather than a high-energy portable battery. >Pero encaja muy bien en almacenamiento estacionario para parques solares que cargan durante el día y descargan por la noche, o centros de datos que necesitan respaldo inmediato cuando se cae la red. Usar materiales abundantes (níquel, hierro, carbono, proteínas) y una vida útil de décadas puede abaratar el coste total de infraestructura de almacenamiento renovable frente a tecnologías que hay que reemplazar con más frecuencia. En resumen, han “resucitado” la química robusta y de bajo impacto de Edison, pero usando nanotecnología y biotemplates para obtener una batería rapidísima y casi “eterna” en ciclos, pensada como almacén de energía para la transición renovable más que como batería portátil de alta energía. Hello Steemit Community, My name is a 28-year-old young man from Iraq. Today, I am not just writing words; I am opening my heart to share the details of a life that many pass by without noticing. My Profession which I am Proud of.. and My Bitter Reality I work as a "sanitation worker" (cleaner) on the streets of Iraq. Every day, before the sun rises, I am there, collecting what others leave behind to keep the face of my city clean. Honest work is not a shame; it is a medal of honor I wear on my chest, but behind this medal is a story of a daily struggle that never ends. The Equation of Survival: Only $200 In a country with so much wealth, I earn a monthly salary of only $200. This amount might seem small to some, but for me, it is the budget for an entire life. It must cover food, water, and basic necessities. In Iraq, we sometimes feel that the system forgets the working class, leaving us to face the high cost of living with bare chests. Why am I here on Steemit? I didn't join this platform just to complain, but because I have a dream that is simple yet big at the same time: To have my own home. My dream is to own four walls that protect me and make me feel safe, away from the constant worry of rent and instability. I have come to this global community to share my journey and the details of my struggle. I believe that your support (through Upvotes and comments) can be the bridge that connects my difficult reality to my dream of building a stable future. A Message to My Country.. and to You It hurts me that my homeland does not give me what I deserve, but I refuse to give up. I will continue to clean the streets with my hands during the day, and build my future with you here at night through my words. Thank you to everyone who reads, and thank you to every hand that reaches out with support to change the life of a person seeking an honest living.

Google has launched its new AI model, Gemini 3.1 Pro. The company claims that this model can solve complex problems with improved reasoning. This is considered a major upgrade over the Gemini 3 Pro that was released three months ago. Tech giant Google has begun rolling out its latest artificial intelligence model, Gemini 3.1 Pro. According to the company, this model is more capable of understanding and solving complex problems than ever before. This update comes exactly three months after Gemini 3 Pro was released on November 19th. What are the improvements in Gemini 3.1 Pro? Google says that Gemini 3.1 Pro has improved "core reasoning," or basic reasoning abilities. The company is presenting it as a model that can handle complex and multi-level questions with greater accuracy. Improved performance in multiple benchmarks The company stated that the new model outperformed the Gemini 3 Pro and other companies' flagship models on several AI benchmarks. Specifically, on the ARC-AGI-2 test, which measures the ability to understand new logic patterns, Gemini 3.1 Pro outperformed rival models. This indicates its adaptability and strong reasoning power. Responds to complex questions According to Google, this model is designed for situations where simple answers aren't enough. It solves multi-layered questions and complex tasks using advanced reasoning techniques. It can be used to visually explain complex topics, present big data in an integrated way, and support creative workflows. Which users will have access? Gemini 3.1 Pro is being released to all users through the Gemini app. However, subscribers to Google AI Pro and Google AI Ultra plans will receive higher usage limits. This model is also available in NotebookLM, but access there will be limited to Pro and Ultra users only. With this launch, Google has made it clear that strengthening reasoning capabilities is its priority in the race for generative AI.

明天就是初八了，大部分人都要开始上班了。 这段时间和娃在家里是玩的不亦乐乎，之前还去万达大玩家玩了很多游戏设备，也因此得了很多积分。 我原本用2万积分兑换了一个尺寸不小的东方明珠乐高积木，其实积分够换两个不一样的款式。 但娃坚持要把剩下的积分留着，等商家更新礼品后，再来换他更心仪的建筑系列乐高，我便答应了他，所以这次只换了这一个东方明珠。 说是积分兑的礼品，可羊毛终究出在羊身上，没有花钱买游戏币，哪来的这么多积分呢？ 虽然过年期间有活动，30多块钱能买100个币，但这些币不到半个小时就用完了。游戏厅的赚钱机制很有一套，设备玩一次就要3个币甚至更多，看似二三十块买了一两百个币，用起来却格外快。 这个东方明珠乐高是小颗粒款式，娃整整花了半个白天加一个晚上才拼完，而且全程都是他独立完成的。拼底座时有些颗粒太小不好拼接，他居然从抽屉的工具箱里翻出一个大锤子，每拼一下就用锤子锤平，那模样把我逗得哈哈大笑。 我不禁感慨，他如果搞学习能有拼乐高这么专注和认真，该有多好呀。 大人们明天初八开工，孩子们大概也只剩一周就要开学了。得赶紧让他自己合计合计寒假作业的完成情况，还好除了《水浒传》的课外书批注和导读单没写完，其他都已经搞定，不用临时抱佛脚。明后天开始，培训机构也会陆陆续续开课，生活又要回归繁忙的节奏了。

When we visit a website or open a mobile app, everything looks simple and beautiful on the screen. We tap a button, log into an account, or search for something, and instantly we get a result. It feels smooth and effortless. But have you ever wondered what actually happens behind the screen? That invisible system working silently is called backend technology. Backend is the part of a website or application that users cannot see. It is responsible for handling data, managing servers, connecting databases, and making sure everything works properly. In simple words, if the frontend is the face of an application, the backend is its brain and nervous system. For example, when you log into your social media account, your username and password are not checked on your phone. Instead, that information is sent to a server. The backend verifies your details from a database and then allows access to your account. All of this happens within seconds. Without backend technology, logging in would not be possible. Backend development usually involves three major elements: servers, databases, and application logic. The server receives requests from users. The application logic decides what should happen with those requests. The database stores important information like user profiles, posts, messages, or transaction records. These three components work together to create a functional system. There are many programming languages used for backend development. JavaScript with Node.js is popular because it allows fast and scalable applications. Python is widely used for its simplicity and readability. PHP powers many websites around the world. Java is often chosen for large enterprise-level systems because of its stability. The choice of technology depends on the project’s needs, budget, and scalability requirements. One of the most important responsibilities of backend technology is security. In today’s digital world, data protection is critical. User passwords must be encrypted. Payment information must be secured. Systems must be protected from hackers and cyber attacks. A weak backend can cause serious damage to a company’s reputation and user trust. Performance is another key factor. Imagine an e-commerce website during a big sale. Thousands of users may visit the site at the same time. The backend must handle all those requests without crashing. This is why scalability and optimization are very important in backend development. Modern backend systems also use cloud computing. Instead of running on a single physical server, applications are hosted on cloud platforms that can automatically adjust resources based on traffic. This makes systems more flexible and cost-efficient. APIs are another important concept. They allow different applications to communicate with each other. For example, when you log into a website using Google or Facebook, the backend connects with their systems through APIs. Becoming a backend developer requires strong problem-solving skills. It is not just about writing code. Developers must understand how systems communicate, how databases are structured, and how to optimize performance. Logical thinking and attention to detail are very important in this field. Although backend work is invisible to users, it is the foundation of every successful digital platform. From online banking systems to social media networks, everything depends on a powerful backend structure. Without it, websites would simply not function. In conclusion, backend technology is the silent force that keeps the digital world running. While users interact with beautiful interfaces, backend systems ensure speed, security, and reliability. As technology continues to grow, the demand for skilled backend developers will continue to rise. It may not always be visible, but backend is truly the heart of modern software development.

Wearable devices track: Sleep quality Heart rate Daily movement Stress levels People are focusing on: Clean nutrition Mental well-being Digital detox Work-life balance Folks working today in cities like New York or London, also many throughout Europe, know full well how much burnout can cost. Though quiet at first, the toll shows up in time, health, even focus - adding up faster than most expect. Folks now care more about living long lives while staying charged up each day. What matters most has shifted toward lasting strength and daily spark. 3. Career Flexibility Is Power The traditional 9-to-5 structure is evolving. Work that mixes home and office is spreading worldwide. So is hiring people by the job, not the hour. Running your own business also keeps rising everywhere. Smart professionals are: Building multiple income streams Investing in digital skills Networking internationally Leveraging AI tools A person built for what comes next bends without breaking. Flexibility shapes how they move through change. Career safety now depends on more than a single company. Learning never stops. 4. Financial Intelligence Matters Fear of rising prices, shaky world trade, sudden shifts - people now handle money differently. People watch every move the economy makes, cautious, adjusting without announcement or fanfare. People in europe and north america Investing earlier Learning about crypto and digital assets Practicing mindful spending Avoiding unnecessary debt A smart lifestyle includes financial literacy. Mental well-being often shifts when money worries step in. Freedom feels different under that weight. 5. Digital Balance Matters Though gadgets make life easier, they pile on stress too. Smart living includes: Scheduled offline time Curated social media usage Prioritizing real-life relationships Creating focused work hours Sometimes wisdom shows up by stepping away. Quiet moments hold answers louder than noise ever could. 6. Sustainability Is the Future Climate awareness is influencing lifestyle choices worldwide. Consumers prefer: Sustainable brands Ethical production Minimal waste Renewable energy solutions Living well isn’t only tied to individual achievement but also shaped by choices that ripple beyond oneself, often quietly influencing daily rhythms in ways unnoticed at first glance yet clear over time when viewed from a distance without filters or noise it is about global responsibility. A fresh rhythm of living could favor people planning far ahead. What comes next might smile on anyone looking past now. Conclusion Faster ways to live are now shaping how people do things every day. Suddenly, it's clear - success isn’t just about money or rank anymore. Defined through: Health Flexibility Financial stability Emotional intelligence Sustainable thinking Living ahead of time means choosing each step carefully. Technology can enhance life - Yet guidance shapes its path. In today’s shifting landscape, the smartest investment you can make is in yourself.

Executive Summary Apple has officially released macOS Tahoe 26.3, marking the third major update to the macOS Tahoe operating system cycle. The update arrives approximately two months after version 26.2 and primarily concentrates on system stability, bug fixes, and security improvements rather than introducing new user-facing features. While incremental in nature, the release reinforces Apple’s broader software strategy: prioritizing ecosystem reliability and long-term platform security between larger feature-driven updates. Key Developments Version: macOS Tahoe 26.3 Release Date: February 11, 2026 Focus: Bug fixes and security updates Availability: Downloadable via System Settings → Software Update Next Update: macOS Tahoe 26.4 expected to enter testing soon Unlike feature-heavy releases, version 26.3 is described as a maintenance-focused update, addressing underlying system issues and strengthening macOS security architecture. Technical Overview Maintenance updates such as macOS Tahoe 26.3 typically include: Kernel-level stability improvements Patch deployment for newly discovered vulnerabilities Performance optimizations across system services Compatibility refinements for third-party applications Although Apple has not disclosed granular technical details publicly, such releases are critical in preserving system integrity across enterprise and consumer environments. Strategic Analysis Apple’s incremental update cycle reflects a disciplined software governance model. By separating stability patches from major feature introductions, the company ensures: Reduced risk of instability in production environments Faster response to emerging security threats Improved lifecycle management for enterprise users This strategy aligns with Apple’s long-term positioning of macOS as a secure, productivity-focused platform increasingly integrated with AI and cross-device continuity features. Competitive Landscape In the broader desktop operating system market, regular security-focused releases enhance Apple’s competitive standing against alternative platforms that may experience slower patch cycles or fragmented update adoption. By maintaining centralized control over both hardware and software, Apple preserves: Uniform update distribution Higher adoption rates Consistent security standards across its installed base Such advantages remain a defining characteristic of Apple’s ecosystem strategy. Market Implications Although maintenance releases rarely generate consumer excitement, they hold substantial importance for: Enterprise IT departments Developers Security-conscious users Stable update cycles increase institutional trust, which indirectly strengthens hardware sales and enterprise adoption. Moreover, consistent reliability supports Apple’s premium pricing model by reinforcing perceived product quality. Future Outlook Apple is expected to begin testing macOS Tahoe 26.4 in the coming weeks. Unlike version 26.3, the upcoming release is anticipated to introduce new features, potentially including: Expanded Siri functionality Additional emoji sets Further ecosystem integrations If Apple integrates deeper AI enhancements into 26.4, it could signal a broader evolution of macOS toward more intelligent and adaptive user experiences. Conclusion macOS Tahoe 26.3 may not introduce headline-grabbing features, but it plays a critical role in strengthening platform stability and security. For enterprise environments and long-term macOS users, such updates reinforce Apple’s reputation for disciplined software lifecycle management. The real strategic momentum, however, may arrive with macOS Tahoe 26.4—where feature expansion and AI-driven enhancements are expected to take center stage.

результаты анализа надёжности паролей, генерируемых большими языковыми моделями и AI-ассистентами. Исследователи попросили модели Claude, ChatGPT и Gemini сгенерировать надёжный 16-символьный пароль и во всех случаях получили результат, на первый взгляд отвечающий всем требованиям к безопасным паролям и признаваемый утилитами для проверки качества паролей как надёжный. Пароли сочетали в себе символы в разных регистрах, спецсимволы и числа, но лишь выглядели безопасными, а на деле имели минимальную энтропию, формировались по типовому шаблону и при повторных запросах образовывали закономерность. По данным исследователей, сгенерированные большими языковыми моделями предсказуемые пароли используются на практике реальными пользователями и предлагаются в процессе работы над кодом AI-ассистентами. Уровень энтропии в сгенерированных AI-моделями паролях оценивается в 20-27 бит, что требует для подбора пароля от нескольких секунд до часов, в то время как анализ результатов утилитами для проверки качества пароля прогнозирует время подбора в несколько веков. Шаблонность подобных паролей является следствием построения контента большими языковыми моделями на основе предсказания токенов. Из 50 сгенерированных в Claude Opus 4.6 паролей, 18 полностью повторялись, все пароли начинались с буквы (в основном "G"), следом всегда шла цифра (в основном 7), во всех паролях присутствовали символы "L", "9", "m", "2", "$" и "#". В GPT-5.2 почти все пароли начинались на букву "v", после чего в половине паролей следовала буква "Q" и повторяющийся шаблон из ограниченного набора символов. В Gemini 3 почти половина паролей начиналась с символов "K" или "k", после которых чаще всего следовали "#", "P" или "9", набор используемых символов был сильно урезан. Увеличение "температуры" при обращении к AI-моделям ощутимо не влияет на качество генерируемых паролей. Что касается используемых при разработке AI-ассистентов, то во многом качество пароля зависит от сформированного разработчиком запроса. Например, Claude Code c Opus 4.6 и Gemini-CLI с Auto Gemini 3 на запрос сформировать надёжный пароль запускали для генерации пароля команду "openssl rand". При этом Gemini-CLI с Auto Gemini 3 на запрос "сгенерируй пароль" использовал "openssl rand", а на запрос "предложи пароль" - формировал пароль через AI-модель. Codex c GPT-5.3-Code временами запускал внешнюю утилиту для генерации надёжного пароля, но временами генерировал предсказуемый пароль собственными силами. Claude Code c Opus 4.5 чаще всего самостоятельно генерировал предсказуемые пароли. Браузер ChatGPT Atlas при придумывании пароля для регистрации на сайте формировал ненадёжный пароль при помощи AI-модели. Источник: ****

I made some impulse buys after getting a new scanner and picked up several batches of slides a while back from Goodwill and eBay. These pictures span from as early as the late 1940s to as late as the early 1990s. There are thousands of these slides. I will be scanning some from time to time and posting them here mainly because I find them an interesting way to look back at the past. I don't know a whole lot about the ultimate origins of this batch of slides other than they came from Goodwill. Like previous batches, these too often have a date stamped on them that I assume indicates when the film was developed and I believe in most cases should give approximate dating for the photograph itself. So far these seem like mostly vacation photos that were taken in the 1960s and 1970s. I've identified places in Italy, France, Mexico, and Hawaii so far. All of the photos in this set were processed in August 1970. The majority of the photos with that date have been from Italy. However, a few have been from France as I believe at least one of the below photos is. The first photo shows a statue but for some reason the slide has "time out" written on it. No idea why. The second photo looks like the outdoor portion of a restaurant or café, probably in Florence but could be elsewhere in Italy. The individual in the photo have shown up in previous sets so I assume they are related to or at least known by the photographer. The third photo shows a hotel with what are probably other hotels under construction in the distance. I would assume this was taken somewhere in Italy but it could also be France. The last photo is the one I am pretty sure was taken in France. There is a sign that refers to The Medieval City. This refers to Carcassonne which is in southern France. processed August 1970 processed August 1970 processed August 1970 processed August 1970 See the previous post in this series here . The entire collection that has been scanned and uploaded so far can also be found here . We live in an age where the only constant is change. Whether it’s a shifting job market, unpredictable health news, or the daily swirl of social media, uncertainty can feel like a heavy fog that clouds our thoughts and drains our energy. The good news? You don’t need a Ph.D. in psychology to navigate it. Below are a handful of practical, low‑maintenance strategies that anyone can adopt to keep the anxiety of the unknown at bay. 1. Anchor Yourself in Routine When the external world feels chaotic, a personal routine becomes a reliable internal compass. Choose three to five “anchor activities”—a morning stretch, a mid‑day walk, a nightly journaling session—and commit to them. The predictability of these small habits signals to your brain that, even if everything else is fluid, you still have control over a slice of your day. 2. Practice “Micro‑Mindfulness” Full‑blown meditation can feel daunting when you’re already stretched thin. Instead, try micro‑mindfulness: pause for 30 seconds, focus on your breath, and notice the sensations in your hands or feet. Repeating this three to five times a day resets the nervous system, lowers cortisol, and sharpens your capacity to respond rather than react to uncertainty. 3. Limit Information Overload Staying informed is smart, but binge‑scrolling news feeds amplifies the unknown. Designate specific windows—say, 8 a.m. and 6 p.m.—to catch up on reliable sources, then switch off. Turning off non‑essential notifications creates mental space for clearer thinking and reduces the “what‑if” spiral. 4. Reframe the Narrative Our brains love stories, and we often cast uncertainty as a villain. Flip the script: view ambiguous moments as opportunities for growth, creativity, or discovery. When a project’s scope changes, ask yourself, “What new skill could I develop?” This subtle shift transforms stress into curiosity, which is far more motivating. 5. Connect with a “Stress‑Buddy” Sharing worries with a trusted friend or colleague lightens the load. Choose a “stress‑buddy” who can listen without judgment and offer perspective. Even a quick 10‑minute check‑in can remind you that you’re not navigating the unknown alone, and it often sparks practical solutions you hadn’t considered. 6. Embrace Physical Movement Exercise isn’t just a physical antidote; it’s a neurological one. A brisk 15‑minute walk, a quick yoga flow, or a set of bodyweight squats releases endorphins that counteract the fight‑or‑flight response triggered by uncertainty. You don’t need a marathon—just consistent movement to keep stress hormones in check. 7. Set “Flexible” Goals Instead of rigid, outcome‑based targets, craft goals that accommodate change. For example, rather than “launch the product by June 1,” set “complete three prototype iterations by the end of May.” Flexible goals give you a sense of progress while allowing room for inevitable pivots. Closing Thought Uncertainty will always be part of the human experience, but how we respond to it is a skill we can cultivate. By grounding yourself in routine, using bite‑sized mindfulness, curating your information diet, reframing challenges, leaning on supportive peers, moving your body, and setting adaptable goals, you create a resilient interior that can weather any external storm. Give yourself permission to try one or two of these tactics today—small steps often lead to the biggest peace of mind. ***

Steem Hop'—the call of the sport makes me feel a bizarre experience of well-being. I've been addicted to this game since the very start. When I sit down right down to play this amusing game of crossing the road, I don't realize what number of hours have passed! However, because of my busy schedule, I don't have the opportunity to play games like I used to. But today I thought, no, when I get some time today, I'll play the game a little longer. Let's see how many points I can collect by playing until the end today and how far I can pull my rank up on the leaderboard! Let's share the entire journey of today's gameplay with you. I opened the game and went straight to the dashboard. I felt good at first, because as soon as I logged in, I got 10 points of 'Daily Reward'! I saw on the dashboard that I was at level 5 (Explorer), and my current rank was 52. Then I thought, let's see how much luck is helping me today! I went to the spin wheel. As I spun the wheel, I felt a slight excitement in my chest... After stopping, I saw, oh wow! I won another 55-point bonus in one go! When the beginning was so good, it was clear that the gameplay would be great today. The points were collected; now it's time for the real fight. As soon as the game started, the familiar atmosphere appeared on the screen. "STEEM HOP" is written in a big 3D style. And my little chicken character was ready to cross the road. The graphics and colors around were so beautiful that playing the game refreshed my mind. As I was moving forward while playing, the real excitement began. On one side, cars were speeding along the road, on the other side, the railway line, and next to it was the river! To cross the river, you had to jump on floating wooden logs. If you move a little this way and that, you would be straight into the water! If you look at a screenshot, you can see that I was at 17 points and balancing on a wooden log. At this time, I really don't have a chance to blink. I was so excited when the car would come, or the log would pass! I was determined to surpass my high score of 73. When I returned to the dashboard after playing for a while, my eyes were really shining with happiness! My total pointshaved reached 2310. But what I was most happy to see was my rank! I was at number 52 before the game started, and by the end of the game, I jumped to number 48! Finally, I entered the top 50. I don't play much because I don't have much time, but the time I spent today has been fully compensated. Overall, today's gameplay experience was amazing. The thrill and joy of accumulating points at every step in the game is hard to describe. For those who haven't played Steem Hop yet, I'd say give it a try;y, there's no better way to spend your time!

El nieto de Gómez, un tal Marcos Santana, sabía que el viejo estaba bien triste porque se le había muerto su toro favorito en la hacienda (dicen que lo picó una culebra). Dicen que cuando le entregó el toro de bronce, le soltó está frase: > "General, aquí le traigo este, que este sí no se le muere" | | |---| | La cara del toro | No es un toro de lidia: Si se fijan bien, el toro es un Jersey (esos que son más de granja), porque a Gómez lo que le gustaba era la ganadería, nada de corridas de toros. | | |---| | Detalles muy bien logrados | Por mucho tiempo, la *** Menteri Dalam Negeri, Tito Karnavian, merespons permohonan Imam Besar Masjid Islamic Center Lhokseumawe, Dr Tgk H Asnawi Abdullah, MA tentang pembangunan masjid kebanggaan masyarakat Kota Lhokseumawe tersebut. Dalam kunjungan Safari Ramadan ke Lhokseumawe, Mendagri dan rombongan melaksanakan salat Isya dan tarawih di Masjid Agung Islamic Center. Mendagri turut didampingi Wakil Gubernur Aceh, Fadhlullah dan Wali Kota Lhokseumawe, Sayuti Abubakar, dan Sekda Aceh, M. Nasir Syamaun. Ketika memberi sambutan, Tito menanggapi permohonan Tgk Asnawi Abdullah untuk membantu penyelesaian Masjid Agung Islamic Center. “Doakan semoga saya bisa membantu melalui jaringan kita, baik jaringan dalam negeri maupun luar negeri,” ujar Mendagri di depan jamaah salat Isya dan tarawih. Sebelumnya, Tgk Asnawi Abdullah menyampaikan beberapa hal kepada Tito dan rombongan, di antaranya harapan agar Pemerintah ikut membantu penyelesaian Masjid Agung Islamic Center. “Jangan sampai setengah abad masjid ini baru selesai,” ujar Tgk Asnawi. Menurutnya, masjid yang digagas tahun 2001 masa Bupati Tarmizi A Karim tersebut berada di bawah Pemerintah Kabupaten Aceh Utara. Namun, seiring dengan lahirnya Pemerintah Kota Lhokseumawe, pengelolaan masjid ikut pun berpindah. “Kelanjutan pembangunan masjid sempat terhenti lama. untuk itu, kami bermohon kepada Pak Mendagri agar bisa membantu mempercepat penyelesaian masjid. Ada beberapa bagian yang harus diselesaikan segera dan diperbaiki, termasuk lantai dua. Atap pun ada yang bocor,” ungkap Tgk Asnawi. Sebelumnya, Mendagri dan rombongan berbuka puasa bersama dan menyerahkan bantuan kepada masyarakat dan anak yatim. Dalam kesempatan tersebut, Wali Kota Sayuti Abubakar menyebutkan bantuan bencana banjir di Lhokseumawe banyak difasilitasi oleh Mendagri. Dalam pertemuan tersebut, Sayuti sempat menyinggung Dana Tranfer ke Daerah yang sempat terdampak kebijakan efisiensi, bisa ditranfer untuk menutupi berbagai kebutuhan di Kota Lhokseumawe, termasuk untuk kelanjutan pembangunan Masjid Agung Islamic Center.[] *** Posted using

**নমস্কার বন্ধুরা। সকলে কেমন আছেন? আজকে প্রায় দুই মাস পরে আমি আবার পুনরায় steemit এ লেখা শুরু করছি। মাঝের এই সময়গুলোতে অনেক কিছু ঘটে গেছে। যা আপনাদের সাথে এখনো পর্যন্ত শেয়ার করা হয়নি। দিন দিন গল্পের ঝুলি বেড়েই চলেছে, কিন্তু বিভিন্ন কারণে আপনাদের সাথে তা শেয়ার করা হয়ে ওঠেনি। তবে এবার মনস্থির করেই নিয়েছি যে আজকে থেকে আবার পুনরায় নিয়মিত আপনাদের সাথে বিভিন্ন গল্প শেয়ার করব। তাহলে চলুন আজকের গল্পকথা শুরু করা যাক।।** *এর আগে আমি বহুবার আপনাদের সাথে আমার বান্ধবীদের আইবুড়ো ভাতের গল্প শেয়ার করেছি। আগে যারা আপনারা নিয়মিত আমার লেখা পড়তেন তারা জানেন যে আমাদের একটা বান্ধবীদের গ্রুপ আছে। সেই গ্রুপের যে কারোর বিয়ে ঠিক হলে আমরা তাকে আইবুড়ো ভাতের নিমন্ত্রণ করতাম। এই ভাবেই আমরা আমাদের বহু বান্ধবীদের আইবুড়ো ভাত খাইয়েছি। তবে দেখতে দেখতে এবার আমার পালাও চলে এলো। যদিও প্রথম আইবুড়ো ভাতের নিমন্ত্রণটা আমি আমাদের সেই বান্ধবীদের গ্রুপের তরফ থেকে পাইনি। কারণ প্রথম আইবুড়ো ভাতটা খেয়েছিলাম পৌষ মাসে। অনেকেই পৌষ মাসটাকে কোন শুভ কাজের জন্য যথার্থ মাস বলে মনে করেন না, তাই এই মাসে শুভ কাজ অনেকেই এড়িয়ে চলে। তবে আমি আমার বান্ধবীদের জানিয়ে রেখেছিলাম তারা যদি আইবুড়ো ভাত খাওয়াতে চাই তাহলে পৌষ মাস থেকে শুরু করতে পারে কারণ মাঘ মাসেই যেহেতু বিয়ে ছিল তাই ওই অল্প কয়েকদিনের মধ্যে পরপর এত খাওয়া সম্ভব হবে না।* *তাই প্রথম নিমন্ত্রণটা পেয়েছিলাম আমার বি.এড কলেজের বান্ধবীদের কাছ থেকে। যদিও ওরা চেয়েছিল আমাকে একটা সারপ্রাইজ দেবে, তবে আমি সেই সারপ্রাইজটা ভেস্তে দিয়েছিলাম। কীভাবে? দাঁড়ান বলছি। আসলে ওরা আমাকে আগে থেকে জানায়নি যে ওরা আমাকে আইবুড়ো ভাত খাওয়াবে। এমনি আমাদের প্ল্যান হয়েছিল রেস্টুরেন্টে গিয়ে একদিন সময় কাটাবো। যেহেতু বিএড কলেজের পর তাদের সাথে আমার নিয়মিত আর দেখা সাক্ষাৎ হয় না তাই মাঝে মাঝেই আমরা এরকম প্ল্যান করি। সেই মতো আমরা একটা দিন ঠিক করেছিলাম রেস্টুরেন্টে যাওয়ার জন্য। তবে যাওয়ার আগের দিন, আমি জানতে পারি যে পরের দিন আমাকে এক জায়গায় যেতেই হবে, তাই আমি ওদেরকে জানাই যে আগামীকাল আমার যাওয়া হবে না। তখন ওরা বাধ্য হয়ে আমাকে বলেই ফেলেছিল যে, "তুই না গেলে তো আমাদের যাওয়ার কোন মানেই হয় না, কারণ আমরা সবাই মিলে তোকে আইবুড়ো ভাত খাওয়াবো।"* *এটা শুনে এত খুশি হয়ে গিয়েছিলাম যে পরের দিনের কাজকর্ম ফেলে আইবুড়ো ভাত খাওয়ার জন্য চলে গিয়েছিলাম। আসলে এতদিন বান্ধবীদের আইবুড়ো ভাত খাইয়েছি, আর এই প্রথমবার আমার আইবুড়ো ভাত হবে এটা ভেবে আমি ভীষণ এক্সাইটেড হয়ে গিয়েছিলাম। তাই, বেশি এক্সাইটমেন্টে পরের দিনের গুরুত্বপূর্ণ কাজও ভুলে গিয়েছিলাম। যেহেতু সেই সময়টাই প্রচন্ড শীত পড়েছিল, তাই ওরা কেউই শাড়ি পরে আসতে চায়নি। যদিও অন্য সময়ে আমরা শাড়ি পরেই দেখা করি। কারণ শাড়ি পরলে বেশ ভালো ভালো ফটো ওঠে। আর আমরা সকলেই ফটো তুলতে খুব ভালবাসি। তবে প্রচন্ড শীতের জন্য ওরা একেবারেই শাড়ি পরতে চাইনি। তবে আইবুড়ো ভাতে আমি ঠিক করেই রেখেছিলাম যে আমি শাড়ি পরবো। তাই ওরা শাড়ি পরে না আসতে চাইলেও আমি কিন্তু শাড়ি পরেই গিয়েছিলাম।* **পরবর্তী পর্বে অবশ্যই আপনাদের সাথে আমার প্রথম আইবুড়ো ভাত খাওয়ার গল্প বিস্তারে বলবো। আজ তাহলে এখানেই শেষ করছি। সকলে ভালো থাকবেন, সুস্থ থাকবেন।**

Or maybe insects But when working with tone curves, you also need to follow the measure. Otherwise, the inversion is either unrecognizable or ugly. In general, it's just an interesting image game :) У термина "инверсия" есть много трактовок. Но суть в том, что основная версия чего-то заменяется на противоположное или просто другое. ***The Hibiscus rosa-sinensis, commonly known as the Chinese hibiscus, is a vibrant flowering plant that captures the very spirit of the tropics. Scientifically classified as Hibiscus rosa-sinensis, this stunning bloom is admired for its large, trumpet-shaped petals and dramatic central staminal column. Its colors range from fiery red and sunny yellow to soft pink, peach, and even multi-toned varieties. Each flower seems to open like a burst of sunlight, bold and unapologetically alive.*** ***Native to East Asia but now grown widely in warm climates around the world, hibiscus thrives in countries with tropical and subtropical weather. In places like Bangladesh, it is a familiar sight in home gardens, lining fences and brightening courtyards. The plant loves sunlight and responds generously to care, producing blooms that last a single day yet appear in continuous succession. This daily renewal gives hibiscus a poetic charm—each morning offers a fresh blossom, as if nature is starting anew.*** ***Beyond its beauty, Hibiscus rosa-sinensis carries deep cultural and symbolic meaning. In many Asian traditions, the flower represents femininity, hospitality, and delicate beauty. In some cultures, it is offered in prayer or worn in the hair as a symbol of grace. Its striking red variety, in particular, is often associated with passion and vitality, standing out boldly against glossy green leaves.*** ***The hibiscus plant is not only ornamental but also practical. In traditional herbal practices, its petals are sometimes used to make teas or natural hair treatments. The flower’s soft texture and rich pigments have inspired art, textiles, and decorative designs for centuries. Even when a bloom falls at the end of its brief life, it leaves behind a quiet reminder of nature’s rhythm—intense beauty does not have to be permanent to be meaningful.*** ---- ---- *Through the lens of @snapsage – capturing moments,telling stories and preserving beauty one shot at a time.* ---- ### Thanks for Reading My Blog Post. ----
